response.justification: The candidate brings directly relevant AI-native experience — current work on MCP code generators, AI-agent infrastructure, and agentic workflows at re:cinq — plus genuine high-throughput credentials from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (microservices handling millions of requests per second). TypeScript is strong, and the breadth across web, embedded, and platform work fits the systems-thinking profile. However, Python — a must-have at 'strong' level — is absent from both the skills list and every role description, most of the low-latency work was in DLang, and evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, and operational/on-call ownership is thin. Worth a conversation to probe Python depth and strategy scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Hands-on AI-native engineering: MCP code generators, agent infrastructure, and agentic workflow tooling in current role
Proven high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise across product, platform, and infrastructure work
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume — a must-have skill listed at 'strong' level
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy, decision-making at scale, or cross-functional stakeholder management
No explicit mention of agile ceremonies, on-call rotation, or production operational ownership
